title: poa cusickii subsp. epilis
intro: Poa cusickii subsp. epilis, also known as Cusick's bluegrass, is a sub-species of grass native to North America, characterized by its blue-green foliage and slender stems. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Poa cusickii subsp. epilis include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues, and support immune function. Poa cusickii subsp. epilis can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
